v. v. P. co.


IS VERY KIND

GRAClOUSLY GIVES PERMtS-
SION FOR CITY TO BUILD A
BRIDGE AT STEVENS ST.
CROSSING.

pages.raw_text:
Officials of the Washington
Water Power Co. this morning
gave their consent for the city to
construct a bridge across the river
on Stevens st. When this state
ment was made by the board OF
works this morning it caused con.
siderable surprise, as it was
thought the city had the right to
the street. 1t seems that 1t has
to the street up to the waters
edge, but not the right to build
piers on the bottom of the river
because that belongs to the power
company.


In the early days the company Sc
cured all Of the river, bed, water
and all, in this township, owing to
a peculiar mistake, either inten.
tional or accidental at Washing.
ton. The river, although meander.
ed the rest of it's length, VISA never
meandered in this township. 1t
was, Of course, but the notes don't
show on the records, 1t having been
stated that they were stolen en-
route to Washington.


OF course the city is much
obliged to the w. w. P. 1t isn't
often the power monopoly gives up
anything. Possibly the company
may eventually permit the city to
built bridge at Monroe St, pro-
viding the city pays the bill.
